title: How I'm scraping by — first month in Austin
html:
TL;DR

A bullet-list inventory of Yahir's survival tactics in his first weeks in austin tx (he arrived Sep 2025 per Memory). Covers the supply side (bridge income strategies) and the demand side (no runway discipline) of running on no buffer. Modified through Dec 2025 — meaning he kept adding to it for months as new tactics emerged.

The note has the same line-item shape as a P&L. No prose. The only deviations from the bullet form are the moments he can't help himself: "I just got mad at myself mentally for spending $5.50 on a blended Horchata. I'm seriously making it out the mud." And: "I'm sitting next to a homeless dude at whole foods, he paid for his meal I finessed mine."

Key quotes

"barber school haircut / shitty $1.5/lbs chicken thighs / absolutely striking deals on fb marketplace / good grace of my mom / good grace of my dad"

"I just got mad at myself mentally for spending $5.50 on a blended Horchata. I'm seriously making it out the mud"

"I am so fucking broke I needed to change my oil for $55 dollars and I know that's going to fuck me"

"Sell my gun at a 50% discount to pay rent and ask my dad embarrassingly for an advance"

"Plasma donation?!!"

Why it matters in the graph

  • Ground truth for the Memory line: "Self-funded, no runway buffer. Has to survive AND build simultaneously."
  • The lived consequence of the founder-philosophy he was writing in 2022: voluntary adversity is no longer abstract.
  • The question mark on "Plasma donation?!!" is the closest thing to a cry for help in the corpus so far. Worth flagging.
  • Confirms whole foods, heb, market of choice as a connected eating-tactic cluster across geographies.

Open questions

  • Did the food-stamps application come through?
  • Has any of the post-Dec-2025 amendment to this note been ingested elsewhere?
  • Did Yahir ever do the plasma donation?

Wikilinks

bridge income strategies · no runway discipline · austin tx · whole foods · heb · market of choice · planet fitness · doordash · fb marketplace · rankett · voluntary adversity · fear of not doing · Memory
